slides.headline: Building a sync-ready catalogue isn't a weekend project.
slides.eyebrow: Education · How to build a sync-ready catalogue from scratch
fit_warnings: [01 hook.eyebrow] copy (60 chars) exceeds slot budget (48 at 44px) — needs a rewrite

slides.body: But it's not complicated. It's sequential.
Here's the exact 5-step process we use at SyncMaster — from application to brief-ready. ↓

STEP 1: RIGHTS AUDIT (Weeks 1–2)
Before anything else: confirm what you own.
→ Is every composition 100% original? One uncleared sample stops the whole catalogue.
→ Who controls the master? You, a label, or joint ownership?
→ Is publishing registered with a PRO?
→ Any co-writers? Get the split documented in writing now.
fit_warnings: [04 context.body] copy (316 chars) exceeds slot budget (206 at 48px) — needs a rewrite

slides.body:
STEP 2: FILE PREPARATION (Weeks 2–3)
→ Export full lossless WAV for every sync-ready track
→ File naming convention: Artist_Title_BPM_Key.wav
→ Export stems for every session: drums, bass, harmony, melody, atmosphere
→ Store stems in a cloud folder — accessible within 10 minutes
fit_warnings: [05 context.body] copy (275 chars) exceeds slot budget (206 at 48px) — needs a rewrite

slides.body:
STEP 3: METADATA TAGGING (Week 3)
→ ISRC assigned to every recording
→ BPM, key, duration, genre embedded in file metadata
→ Mood tags: 3–5 per track in industry vocabulary
→ PRO affiliation and composer information embedded correctly STEP 4: CATALOGUE INDEXING (Week 4)
→ Build a simple spreadsheet: title, BPM, key, mood, instrumentation, one-stop status
→ Separate into categories: brand/corporate, cinematic, documentary, emotional
→ Note which tracks have complete stems vs. partial stems STEP 5: RESPONSE SYSTEM (Week 4)
→ Set brief notification to reach you within 1 hour of receipt
→ Test your delivery process: how fast can you send stems for 3 tracks?
→ Confirm rights for every catalogue track so you can respond in writing immediately This process takes 3–5 weeks done properly.
Every brief you respond to after that takes a fraction of the time. #SyncReady #AfricanComposers #SyncMaster #MusicBusiness
